tone_examples: Quick check — your repeat-prescription customer count is up 18% this month
Heads up: a generic alternative for {molecule} just got approved — likely 30% lower MRP

offer_catalog.title: Annual Health Card @ ₹399 (15% off all year)

digest.title: Generic metformin SR price drop after 4 new approvals
digest.summary: Three major Indian players got SR-formulation approval; wholesale price down 22% effective next month. Branded retail likely flat for now (margin will absorb).
digest.actionable: Audit your shelf — switching diabetic refills to generic now saves the patient ~₹120/month and improves your repeat-buy stickiness

digest.title: FDA enforcement audit on Schedule H1 antibiotic dispensing — Q2
digest.summary: Tighter Q2 audit cycle on H1 antibiotic dispensing — proper Rx capture, register entries, batch tracking. ₹50,000+ penalties for missing entries.
digest.actionable: Audit your H1 register; ensure pharmacist signature + Rx photocopy on file for every dispense in last 90 days

digest.title: Summer demand shift: ORS, sunscreen, anti-fungal up 40%; cold/cough down 60%
digest.summary: Standard April-Jun pattern: ORS sachets, sunscreen, anti-fungal creams, deodorant peak; respiratory medication tapers off. Restock + shelf-rearrange.
digest.actionable: Move ORS + sunscreen to counter visibility; cold/cough to back shelf

digest.title: Chronic-Rx subscription retention 3.2x higher than walk-in
digest.summary: Pharmacies with WhatsApp-based refill reminder + auto-delivery for chronic patients see 88% 12-month retention vs 27% for walk-in-only chronic customers.
digest.actionable: Set up the WhatsApp reminder — covers diabetic, hypertensive, thyroid Rx

digest.title: Voluntary recall: Specific atorvastatin batches by manufacturer X
digest.summary: Two batches (numbers in alert) flagged for sub-potency. Customers should be informed; replacement available via distributor return chain. No safety risk for patients beyond suboptimal LDL control.
digest.actionable: Pull the batches; WhatsApp affected customers from your repeat-Rx list

patient_content_library.title: Generic vs branded medicines — what's actually different?
patient_content_library.body: The active molecule is identical and regulator-tested. Difference is in the binders, fillers, and brand markup. Generic is typically 40-60% cheaper. Two cases where brand may help: (1) very narrow-therapeutic-index drugs (some thyroid, blood thinners) where bioequivalence varies, (2) extended-release formulations. Otherwise, generic is the rational choice.

patient_content_library.title: Where to NOT store your medicines
patient_content_library.body: Three places: (1) bathroom — humidity wrecks tablet integrity. (2) car glove box — temperature swings degrade most molecules in a month. (3) refrigerator door — temperature varies too much; use the main shelf if cold storage is needed. The kitchen at room temperature, away from sunlight, is best.

patient_content_library.title: Summer first-aid: what every Indian household should have
patient_content_library.body: Five items: ORS sachets (heat-stroke is sudden), broad-spectrum sunscreen SPF 50+ (apply 30 min before sun), anti-fungal cream (sweat triggers infections), Vaseline (chafing relief), and a thermometer. Skip energy drinks marketed as 'summer drinks' — sugar load is counterproductive when dehydrated.

seasonal_beats.note: summer surge — ORS, sunscreen, anti-fungal, deodorant
monsoon — anti-bacterial, anti-fungal, immunity supplements peak
festival sweets → blood sugar spike — diabetic monitoring needs surge
